etymology.description: semantic 艹 ("grass; plant") + phonetic 早 -> originally referred to 皂 -> borrowed for "grass"
etymology.interpretation: The character 草 (cǎo, "grass; herb") combines the semantic 艹 ("grass; plant"), which visually represents vegetation, with the phonetic 早 (zǎo). Although originally linked to the meaning of 皂 (zào) as "acorn," it was later borrowed to denote "grass" due to the semantic component's direct depiction of plant life.

examples.chinese: 草地上有很多花(草地上有很多花)。
examples.pinyin: Cǎodì shàng yǒu hěn duō huā.
examples.english: There are many flowers on the grass.

examples.chinese: 他正在割草(他正在割草)。
春天时(春天時)，草变得非常绿(草變得非常綠)。
examples.pinyin: Chūntiān shí, cǎo biàn dé fēicháng lǜ.
examples.english: In spring, the grass becomes very green.
